popularStory.description: Henry of Scotland (Eanric mac Dabíd, 1114 – 12 June 1152) was a Prince of Scotland, heir to the Kingdom of Alba. He was also Earl of Northumberland and Earl of the Honour of Huntingdon and Northamptio …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria於1114年11月19日出生在蘇格蘭洛包爾凱爾索時，他的父親David I King of Scots34歲，母親Matilda of Huntingdon40歲。他於1139年與Ada de Warenne結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1152年6月12日在他的家鄉享年37歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭洛包爾凱爾索。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria于1114年11月19日出生在苏格兰洛包爾凱爾索时，他的父亲David I King of Scots34岁，母亲Matilda of Huntingdon40岁。他于1139年与Ada de Warenne结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1152年6月12日在他的家乡享年37岁，埋葬在苏格兰洛包爾凱爾索。

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Huntington  .

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Scotland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Kinross and Clackmannanshire):
from the Middle English personal name Scotland, Scolland. Found in Normandy as well as England, it evidently denoted a Scot or someone with an association with Scotland.
ethnic name for someone from Scotland, from Middle English Scotlond ‘Scotland’. Compare England .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria was born on 19 November 1114, in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, his father, David I King of Scots, was 34 and his mother, Matilda of Huntingdon, was 40. He married Ada de Warenne in 1139. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 12 June 1152, in his hometown, at the age of 37, and was buried in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria est né le 19 novembre 1114, à Kelso, Roxburghshire, Écosse, son père, David I King of Scots, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Matilda of Huntingdon, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Ada de Warenne en 1139. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 12 juin 1152, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Kelso, Roxburghshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria am 19. November 1114 in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater David I King of Scots 34 und seine Mutter Matilda of Huntingdon 40. Er hat 1139 Ada de Warenne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 12. Juni 1152 im Alter von 37 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria è nato il il 19 novembre 1114 nel Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, il suo padre, David I King of Scots, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Matilda of Huntingdon, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Ada de Warenne nel 1139. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 12 giugno 1152, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolto a Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbriaが1114年11月19日にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアケルソで生まれた時，父David I King of Scotsは34歳，母Matilda of Huntingdonは40歳であった。1139年にAda de Warenneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1152年6月12日に故郷で37歳で死去し，スコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアケルソにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria(이)가 1114년 11월 19일 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 켈소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David I King of Scots(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda of Huntingdon(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1139년 Ada de Warenne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1152년 6월 12일 그의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 켈소에 묻혔다.

pt.19 November 1114–12 June 1152: 19 de novembro de 1114–12 de junho de 1152
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria nasceu em 19 de novembro de 1114, em Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, seu pai, David I King of Scots, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Matilda of Huntingdon, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Ada de Warenne em 1139. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de junho de 1152, em sua cidade natal, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria родился 19 ноября 1114 г. в местности келсо, роксбургшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени David I King of Scots было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda of Huntingdon, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Ada de Warenne в 1139 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 12 июня 1152 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности келсо, роксбургшир, Шотландия.

es.19 November 1114–12 June 1152: 19 de noviembre de 1114–12 de junio de 1152
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria nació el 19 de noviembre de 1114, en Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, su papá, David I King of Scots, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Matilda of Huntingdon, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Ada de Warenne en 1139. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 12 de junio de 1152, en su pueblo, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultado en Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland.
